title: Weltzeituhr - Quick Summary
items: **The Weltzeituhr is the World Time Clock** on Alexanderplatz, one of Berlin's easiest meeting points.
**It was presented on 30 September 1969** as part of East Berlin's socialist redesign of Alexanderplatz.
**Erich John designed it** with city names, 24 time zones, a rotating hour ring and a solar system model above.
**Its GDR context is the key:** it showed world time in a country where many people could not freely travel.
**Today it is a protected monument and tour starting point** under the TV Tower in the heart of the old East.
